name: New Jersey
tagline: A new governor, a marquee AI Hub anchored by Princeton, CoreWeave and Microsoft, and a first-in-the-nation push to regulate AI data centers — New Jersey is positioning itself as the East Coast's AI center while reshaping how it powers and governs that compute.

execSummary.summary: New Jersey enters mid-2026 under a new administration with a clear technology identity. Governor Mikie Sherrill (D), sworn in January 20, 2026 as the state's 57th governor, inherits the NJ AI Hub — a Princeton-anchored ecosystem with CoreWeave and Microsoft as founding partners that aims to make New Jersey the East Coast's leading AI center. The state runs on a single-year fiscal cycle; the FY2026 budget signed by former Governor Murphy totals $58.78B. Three shifts matter for SLG sellers: the AI Hub and surrounding 'AI corridor' along Route 1 are pulling in compute and talent; Sherrill has unveiled a first-in-the-nation framework to regulate AI data centers (making operators fund their own power and grid upgrades) amid ~20% electric-bill increases driven largely by data-center demand; and central IT leadership is in transition after CTO Chris Rein's retirement, with NJOIT (50+ departments) and the NJCCIC cyber fusion center as the key statewide buyers. For Dell, the AI-infrastructure angle is the headline, balanced against an energy-and-governance climate that rewards efficient, governed compute.
opportunities: AI-infrastructure demand tied to the NJ AI Hub, Princeton/Rutgers research computing, and the Route 1 'AI corridor' (PowerEdge, networking, GPU-dense systems, services).

Governed, on-prem / private-cloud AI for state agencies operationalizing the NJ AI Assistant and AI Task Force recommendations — sensitive workloads that favor controlled compute over open public models.
Cyber resilience build-out with the NJCCIC and the new Civilian Cyber Resilience Corps — secure infrastructure and managed services for state and local government.
Endpoint, client, and lifecycle modernization across 50+ NJOIT-served departments as leadership and priorities reset under a new administration.

stats.label: New power demand from data centers

budget.intro: New Jersey budgets on an annual (single-year) cycle running July 1 to June 30. The FY2026 Appropriations Act, signed by then-Governor Murphy on June 30, 2025, appropriates $58.78B in State funds (plus roughly $31B in federal funds). There is no single 'state IT budget' line; technology spend is distributed across agencies and is centrally coordinated through the Office of Information Technology (NJOIT), with cyber funded through Homeland Security / the NJCCIC. Governor Sherrill delivered her first budget address in early 2026 emphasizing affordability, energy, and accountability for Big Tech and data centers.

stats.label: Fiscal year
stats.note: Annual cycle; FY2027 in formation under Sherrill

notes: Budget figures are annual (single-year) totals; New Jersey does not budget biennially.

Technology and cyber dollars are distributed across NJOIT-served agencies and Homeland Security (NJCCIC) rather than a single consolidated IT appropriation — confirm the owning entity before forecasting.
FY2027 is being shaped under a new administration focused on affordability and energy costs; expect data-center and grid policy to influence tech-adjacent spending.

initiatives.name: NJ AI Hub
initiatives.summary: Announced in January 2025 by then-Governor Murphy and Princeton President Eisgruber, with Microsoft and CoreWeave as founding partners and $72M+ committed across the state (NJEDA), Princeton, and industry. Located at 619 Alexander Road in West Windsor along the Route 1 corridor, the Hub advances three pillars: AI research, commercialization/acceleration, and AI education/workforce. In December 2025 it selected Plug and Play to operate a regional AI Accelerator. The strongest tailwind in the state for Dell AI-infrastructure and research-computing demand.

initiatives.name: AI Data Center Regulatory Framework
initiatives.summary: Governor Sherrill in June 2026 unveiled what her administration calls the country's first comprehensive statewide framework for regulating AI data centers. The four-part plan would make operators fund their own energy supply and grid upgrades, publicly report energy/water use, negotiate community benefit agreements, and use union labor — shifting costs off ratepayers. Related bills (e.g., S680) tie clean-energy mandates and large-load tariffs to data centers. The climate rewards efficient, governed, lower-power compute and creates uncertainty for raw hyperscale buildout.

initiatives.name: NJCCIC & Civilian Cyber Resilience Corps
initiatives.summary: The New Jersey Cybersecurity and Communications Integration Cell (NJCCIC), the state's cyber fusion center within Homeland Security, published a new 2026–2030 strategic plan and in December 2025 launched the Civilian Cyber Resilience Corps — a statewide volunteer initiative to help state, local, and critical-infrastructure entities prevent, withstand, and recover from attacks. Drives demand for secure infrastructure, endpoint protection, and managed services across state and local government.

initiatives.name: Enterprise GenAI for State Government
initiatives.summary: Under EO 346 (2023) New Jersey stood up an AI Task Force and directed NJOIT to govern state AI use. The Office of Innovation launched the NJ AI Assistant — a safe 'sandbox' GenAI platform for state employees — and one of the nation's first comprehensive GenAI training programs. As agencies move from pilot to scale, governed and auditable AI on sensitive data is an opening for private/on-prem compute rather than open public models.

procurement.intro: New Jersey centralizes IT direction in the Office of Information Technology (NJOIT), which oversees infrastructure for 50+ state departments and agencies, while purchasing is run by the Department of the Treasury's Division of Purchase & Property (DPP) through the NJSTART eProcurement marketplace. Most technology buys run through DPP statewide contracts and NASPO ValuePoint cooperative agreements (including Cloud Solutions via a Carahsoft participating addendum).

vehicles.name: NJSTART Statewide Contracts
vehicles.detail: Primary platform. DPP-awarded statewide term contracts searchable and orderable by state, county, and municipal entities. Confirm Dell agreements and reseller paths are current.

vehicles.name: NASPO ValuePoint (cooperative)
vehicles.detail: New Jersey participates in NASPO ValuePoint IT contracts (e.g., Cloud Solutions, IT VMSP) via participating addenda — a fast lane for cooperative IT purchasing.

vehicles.name: NJEDA / partnership vehicles
vehicles.detail: For AI Hub and economic-development-linked compute, the NJ Economic Development Authority and Princeton partnerships can be non-traditional routes alongside standard procurement.

entities.name: NJOIT (Office of Information Technology)
entities.role: Central IT authority and infrastructure provider for 50+ departments; sets statewide tech and AI-governance direction.

entities.name: Division of Purchase & Property (Treasury)
entities.role: Statewide procurement authority; runs NJSTART and awards term contracts.

entities.name: NJCCIC (Homeland Security)
entities.role: Cyber fusion center and security buyer for state and local government.

entities.name: Princeton & Rutgers
entities.role: Large independent research-computing and AI-infrastructure buyers; Princeton anchors the NJ AI Hub.

procurement.competition: New Jersey is a contract-driven, cooperative-friendly market where incumbents hold NJSTART and NASPO agreements across compute, networking, and services. Differentiation comes from AI-infrastructure capability tied to the AI Hub and research universities, energy-efficient/governed compute that aligns with the state's data-center scrutiny, and cyber-ready offerings mapped to NJCCIC — more than on price alone.

powerStructure.name: Mikie Sherrill
powerStructure.title: Governor (57th)
powerStructure.note: Sworn in Jan 20, 2026. Priorities: affordability, energy/permitting reform, and accountability for Big Tech and AI data centers.

powerStructure.name: Vernon Spencer
powerStructure.title: Interim Chief Technology Officer
powerStructure.note: Stepped in after CTO Chris Rein retired (Jan 2026) following 8 years leading NJOIT. Leadership transition in progress.

powerStructure.name: NJ AI Task Force
powerStructure.title: AI policy & governance body
powerStructure.note: Chartered by EO 346; led by chief innovation officer, CTO, and NJEDA CEO. Shapes state AI adoption and guardrails.

powerStructure.name: NJCCIC
powerStructure.title: State cyber fusion center
powerStructure.org: NJ Office of Homeland Security & Preparedness
powerStructure.note: Coordinates statewide cybersecurity; 2026–2030 strategic plan and new Cyber Resilience Corps.

news.title: Gov. Sherrill unveils first-in-the-nation AI data-center regulatory framework
news.impact: Operators would fund their own power and grid upgrades and disclose energy/water use; rewards efficient, governed compute and adds uncertainty to raw hyperscale buildout.

news.title: Sherrill's first budget address targets affordability and Big Tech
news.impact: Signals FY2027 priorities — energy costs, data-center accountability — that shape tech-adjacent spending.

news.title: Mikie Sherrill inaugurated as New Jersey's 57th Governor
news.impact: New administration resets executive priorities; technology direction in transition.

news.title: NJOIT CTO Chris Rein retires; Vernon Spencer named interim CTO
news.impact: Central IT leadership transition; relationship-building window for statewide tech direction.

news.title: NJ AI Hub selects Plug and Play to operate regional AI Accelerator
news.impact: Hub moves from launch to scaling startups and research; deepens the state's AI ecosystem and compute demand.

news.title: NJCCIC launches Civilian Cyber Resilience Corps; releases 2026–2030 plan
news.impact: Expands statewide cyber posture; demand for secure infrastructure and managed services across state and local government.

dellActions: Lead with AI infrastructure: tie Dell ISG / AI Factory offerings to the NJ AI Hub, Princeton/Rutgers research computing, and the Route 1 AI corridor.
Position energy-efficient, governed compute that aligns with New Jersey's data-center scrutiny — frame Dell as the partner for high-performance AI that minimizes power and grid burden.
Engage NJOIT during its leadership transition (interim CTO Vernon Spencer) to shape statewide refresh and AI-platform direction before priorities harden under the new administration.
Map cyber offerings to the NJCCIC and Civilian Cyber Resilience Corps for state and local secure-infrastructure and managed-services demand.
Validate Dell coverage on NJSTART statewide contracts and NASPO ValuePoint participating addenda so targeted agencies can buy without a fresh RFP.
